code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of airport‑related triples into a readable, single‑sentence description."""
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Mapping predicates to clause fragments (subject omitted)
    clause_map = {
        "cityServed": "serves {object}",
        "location": "is located in {object}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "lies {object} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {object}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {object} metres",
        "runwayName": "has runway {object}",
        "country": "is in {object}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {object}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway {object} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway {object} metres long",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has runway number {object}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "runway surface is {object}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "second runway surface is {object}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "third runway surface is {object}",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "fourth runway surface is {object}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "fifth runway surface is {object}",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway {object} metres long",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway {object} feet long",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "has a third runway {object} feet long",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "has a fourth runway {object} feet long",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "has fifth runway number {object}",
        # add more as needed
    }

    # Group clause bodies by their subject to avoid repetitive subject mentions
    subject_groups: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        tmpl = clause_map.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            body = tmpl.format(object=t.object)
        else:
            # generic fallback
            body = f"{t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')} {t.object}"
        subject_groups.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(body)

    # Build a clause for each subject
    subject_clauses: list[str] = []
    for subj, bodies in subject_groups.items():
        if len(bodies) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {bodies[0]}"
        else:
            # join multiple bodies with commas and an 'and' before the last one
            clause = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(bodies[:-1]) + f", and {bodies[-1]}"
        subject_clauses.append(clause)

    # Combine subject clauses into one sentence (semicolon separated if more than one subject)
    if len(subject_clauses) == 1:
        sentence = subject_clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = "; ".join(subject_clauses) + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
